conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of object X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. My goal is to sell this object for a profit, meaning I need to receive more than 40 ZUP from Player BLUE. Since this is the first proposal, I should set an asking price that is reasonably above my cost but attractive enough to encourage a deal. I will start by asking for 55 ZUP. This gives me a 15 ZUP profit margin while leaving room for negotiation if the buyer counters. I have 3 proposal attempts total, so I can afford to start slightly high. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 1 unit of resource X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 55 ZUP. This is a fair price that allows me to cover my costs and make a small profit. Let me know if you accept or if you would like to counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED, I appreciate your offer. However, I believe 50 ZUP is a bit low considering your production cost of 40 ZUP. I'm willing to offer 50 ZUP for 1 X. Let me know what you think. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 50 ZUP. This is 10 ZUP above my production cost of 40 ZUP, which is a profit. However, I initially asked for 55 ZUP. Since I have one more proposal left before I must accept or reject, I should try to meet in the middle to secure a better profit margin without risking a rejection. The midpoint between my ask (55) and their offer (50) is 52.5. Since I can only trade in integers, I will round up to 53 ZUP. This gives me a 13 ZUP profit, which is better than their 10 ZUP offer, but still shows I am willing to negotiate. If they reject 53, I can accept 50 on my next turn if I must, or reject if I feel 50 is too low (though 10 profit is decent). I'll propose 53. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 53 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your perspective, Player BLUE. While 50 ZUP is a profit for me, I was hoping for something closer to my original asking price of 55 ZUP. Let's meet in the middle. I am willing to sell you the X for 53 ZUP. This allows us both to walk away happy. Does this work for you? </message>

<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>

This offer gives me a profit of 18 ZUP. Since I have already used 2 proposals and only have 1 left, and this offer is better than anything I have asked for so far, there is no strategic reason to propose a new trade. I should accept this offer immediately to secure the best possible deal. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That is an excellent offer, Player BLUE! 58 ZUP is better than my initial asking price. I am happy to accept this deal. We have a trade! </message>
